title: USD Schema and Schema Fallback Spec Coverage Ledger

normative_source.rule: Only the AOUSD core specification at aousd/specifications-public v1.0.1 is normative for this ledger. OpenUSD behavior is not a source for these requirements.

status_values.implemented: In scope for this schema namespace-source contract and covered by goldens.
status_values.partial: A bounded spec-backed subset is covered; remaining subfeatures are explicitly deferred.

coverage_rules: Schema claims require an explicit schema-registry input because AOUSD v1.0.1 leaves schema definition ingestion implementation-defined.
Schema fallback defaults must remain schema-defined fallback data, not authored layer opinions.
Stage population may consume schema-injected properties from a namespace source but must not implement schema lookup locally.

obligations: Treat schema definition registration as an explicit implementation boundary.
Do not infer registry content from OpenUSD or parser-local syntax.
Use composed typeName to select a registered concrete typed schema.
Compose base typed schema properties as weaker prim-definition properties.
Leave prims typeless when no registered concrete type applies and no fallbackPrimTypes substitute is available.
Read composed apiSchemas field data as applied schema tokens.
Inject single applied schema properties.
Substitute recorded instance names into multiple applied schema property names.
Preserve transitive applied schema presence through schema inclusions.
Merge schema-defined prim-definition properties with authored property children.
Sort the merged property set by path element ordering.
Apply strongest propertyOrder over the merged property set.
Provide typed-schema fallback fields stronger than applied-schema fallback fields.
Provide applied-schema fallback fields in composed applied schema order.
Preserve default metadata empty-sentinel fallback as the weaker value-resolution fallback when no schema default exists.
Read fallbackPrimTypes from composed layer metadata made visible through the namespace source.
Use the preference-ordered fallback type list to select a registered concrete typed schema for an otherwise unregistered typed prim.
Do not reinterpret fallbackPrimTypes as layer-level schema semantics.
Compose registered built-in schemas and registered auto-applies as weaker schema inclusions in the owning schema's prim definition.
Propagate multiple applied schema instance names through unqualified built-ins and prefix owner instances for named multiple applied built-ins.
Apply override properties as sparse stronger metadata opinions over included properties while ignoring typeName and variability override fields.
Emit diagnostics when an override property does not target an included property or violates the included property kind or typeName.
